company_type: Water Quality and Product Quality & Innovation Solutions

dependencies: Wide variety of raw materials including metallic-based components, electronic components, chemistries, OEM products, plastics and other petroleum-based products
For some components that require particular specifications or regulatory qualifications there may be a single supplier or a limited number of suppliers
Supply chain disruptions and labor availability constraints experienced since 2021
risks: Supply chain disruptions due to supplier capacity constraints, transportation and logistics issues, fluctuations in demand, decreased availability of key raw materials or commodities
Legislative or regulatory changes affecting supply
Bankruptcy or exiting of business by suppliers
External events such as natural disasters, pandemic health issues, war, terrorist actions and governmental actions
Single or limited source suppliers for certain requirements due to quality assurance, regulatory requirements, cost effectiveness, availability or uniqueness of design
Interruptions in supply or increases in cost of key components, raw materials, other commodities and labor
Extended lead times during market upturn
Price increases and supply limitations from suppliers
Labor availability constraints and labor cost increases

regulatory_risks: Data privacy and security laws including GDPR in European Union with fines up to €20 million or 4% of total worldwide annual turnover
State privacy laws in California and other U.S. states with similar requirements
Potential federal privacy requirements in the United States
Countries requiring personal data to be maintained on local servers (China, Russia)
Environmental, health and safety laws and regulations concerning generation, storage, use and transportation of hazardous materials, emissions or discharges into environment
Chemical constituents in products and end-of-life disposal and take-back programs
Antitrust laws in U.S. federal, state and international jurisdictions
Export/import control and economic sanctions laws including International Traffic in Arms Regulations, Export Administration Regulations, OFAC regulations
Product and other regulatory approvals in foreign jurisdictions
Compliance requirements for companies doing business with government entities
Rules of industrial standards bodies such as International Standards Organization
Climate change and sustainability legal or regulatory measures
Tax law changes relating to multinational corporations including OECD base erosion and profit shifting initiatives
Changes in tax laws and regulations including potential changes to U.S. Tax Cuts and Jobs Act

sanction_risks: Company suspended shipment of products to Russia in 2022
Exposure to economic sanctions regulations administered by U.S. Department of Treasury Office of Foreign Assets Control
Limited business dealings in countries subject to comprehensive sanctions
Export control restrictions on certain products, services and technologies
Trade protection measures, tariffs, embargoes and import or export restrictions
Russia's reduction of natural gas exports to Europe creating uncertainty on prices and supply for European manufacturing facilities
